Okay, and so they can see the address section and if they flip it over they can see the bottom of page 4. And so that means to be really interesting, really good content. Depending on your market that could be fun or funny. It needs to have a really good headline and if at all possible it should continue inside the newsletter as well. So they can't finish reading the article without opening it. If you'll do those things and if you'll focus on readership in the first few months, you will have a much more profitable experience overall with your newsletter. Fantastic. So obviously this is something you're probably going to have to listen to again or go and read the transcript. Because that was a ton of information. So thank you for dissecting it literally page by page. That's great. So again, don't screw around with the model that he just gave you. He tested this literally thousands and thousands and thousands of times and what he's giving you here is what works. There's no reason to reinvent the wheel. You just heard from the master how to put together your newsletter. So if you follow exactly like that and follow his advice, you're going to get great results. Sean, let me ask you just a couple of other questions. What are some of the best ways to get a direct ROI on your newsletter? Okay, yes. So the way we use it in our business and we recommend to our clients is with a free-standing insert is the easiest way to sell in the newsletter. So a free-standing insert is exactly what it sounds. It's an extra page or half a page slipped into the middle of the newsletter. It's not attached. It's not like bound in there by a staple or anything. It's just it's free-standing. This allows you to provide great content and great value, but without hurting the newsletter by pitching directly in it or pitching too hard. There are definitely ways to pitch like with. We didn't really go over it entirely, but you can talk about a success story. That could be the business section at the bottom of page three or the bottom of page two is a success story. Someone who tried XYZ product and had a great experience. So that's one way of doing it. But the free-standing inserts what we use probably 90, 95% of the time. And what we do is we actually a lot of people don't realize you can do this, but we will segment the list. So for example, our newsletter is getting ready to mail and we've got one free-standing insert going to clients, which has an offer on it. A free-standing insert going to prospects, which has a different offer on it. A free-standing insert going to a dentist happened to be a big market for us. And so they actually have a completely different offer than the normal prospect offer. So these are dentists who are not clients. And let me think here, they may even be doing a fourth one this month. And I just can't recall it off top of my head, but sometimes we even segmented into four different free-standing inserts and offers. And so you can segment the list and send different people different offers. And we've just, I mean, we've done really, really well. So as you mentioned at the beginning of the call, we won the GKC Marketer of the Year. Well, some people on our list have no idea what that is, but it's a very impressive title. So we publicize this on a free-standing insert and put an offer in there of our Marketer of the Year offer or whatever. And for us, it just ended, but I believe the final number was 14 new clients that we got from this free-standing insert, which is a lot for us because we kind of shoot for 20, 25 new clients a month is what we're looking for. And so for that one promotion to provide 14 is a good promotion. Sometimes it does less. Sometimes we do run a promotion that provides four. And sometimes it does more. I think our highest was like 21 or something like that from the free-standing insert. So it's different, but we have different offers for different people, different segments of the list. That is a great way to do it. You should probably be putting tracking numbers on the newsletter. They're pretty inexpensive and it will allow you to see people are calling in and depending on the tracking number you get, you can have recordings on there so you can even hear the call that came in. So you're talking about tracking phone number. Tracking phone number. Sorry, tracking phone number. Yeah, absolutely. And you can get that from the ROI guy. So if you call up GKIC and ask for the ROI guy, you can get you a contact number. Yeah, but that's a great way to go about tracking as well. So you can do an offer where it's like, hey, go download XYZ free report. I prefer to use that as a free-standing offer and take more space and more time because if I, or free-standing insert, I mean...
